levels.body: The simile of the "tears […], like pure crystallines" (7) suggests that the characteristic of crystallines is applied to the watery tears and indicates their being "clear or transparent" ("crystalline, *adj.* and *n.*" A.2.a.) as opposed to the usually solid connotation of a crystal stone ("crystalline, *adj.* and *n.*"A.2.b.) which is later taken up again when tears are made into solid [[amulets]] by God.
worksCited: "crystalline, *adj.* and n." *OED Online.* OUP, June 2011. Web. July 14, 2019.
